Title: Trehalose Biosynthesis Promotes Pseudomonas aeruginosa Pathogenicity in Plants

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycogen synthase family; α-1,4-glucan synthesis enzyme
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes P. aeruginosa growth/virulence in Arabidopsis leaves, likely by facilitating acquisition of nitrogen-containing nutrients
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption of glgA abolishes detectable trehalose production and reduces virulence in Arabidopsis (~20-fold decrease in planta growth), indicating positive contribution to trehalose-dependent pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transposon insertion mutant analysis; in planta bacterial growth assay; trehalose quantification assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial extracellular milieu/intercellular space in infected Arabidopsis leaf (inferred from trans-rescue evidence)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arabidopsis leaf infection; exogenous trehalose supplementation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: In vitro/in planta transcriptional profiling referenced for neighboring cluster regulation; accession NA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Trehalose biosynthesis is required for plant virulence but not metazoan virulence; loss can slightly increase virulence in metazoan hosts at operon level
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antibacterial target to reduce plant infection by opportunistic P. aeruginosa; pathway inhibition may selectively impair plant colonization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. glgA is part of the treYZ operon linked to trehalose biosynthesis. 2. A glgA transposon mutant showed ~20-fold reduced in planta growth in Arabidopsis. 3. glgA disruption eliminated detectable trehalose production, linking glucan precursor formation to trehalose-dependent virulence.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bacterial growth/virulence in Arabidopsis leaves, likely by supporting nitrogen nutrient acquisition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of treS operon reduces trehalose accumulation by ~50% and decreases virulence in Arabidopsis (~5.9-fold for subregion; substantial defect for ΔtreS operon), showing it contributes positively to pathogenic trehalose production
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In-frame deletion mutant; in planta bacterial growth assay; trehalose quantification; exogenous trehalose rescue
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial extracellular milieu/intercellular space in infected Arabidopsis leaf (inferred)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arabidopsis leaf infection; exogenous trehalose; ammonium/nitrate supplementation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of trehalose synthesis does not impair metazoan virulence and may slightly enhance it
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TreS pathway is a candidate metabolic target to limit bacterial plant pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. treS defines a second trehalose biosynthetic route in PA14. 2. ΔtreS lowers trehalose content to about half of wild type. 3. Loss of treS decreases Arabidopsis virulence and this defect is rescued by exogenous trehalose.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts the terminal α-1,4 linkage of a maltooligosaccharide to an α,α-1,1 trehalose-type linkage, generating maltooligosyltrehalose
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; operon is a key control module for trehalose-dependent virulence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The treYZ operon is essential for detectable trehalose production and strongly promotes virulence in Arabidopsis; treY is a central enzymatic component of this route
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Operon deletion (ΔtreYZ); trehalose quantification; Arabidopsis in planta growth assay; trehalose rescue
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports P. aeruginosa pathogenic growth in Arabidopsis leaves, likely via nitrogen acquisition
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arabidopsis leaf infection; exogenous trehalose supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of treYZ abolishes trehalose and severely reduces plant virulence; no requirement inferred for metazoan virulence at double-mutant level
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value antibacterial metabolic target for reducing plant colonization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: treY function is assigned from operon annotation and pathway logic; gene-specific knockout was not separately tested in Results
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. treY is part of the treYZ operon required for trehalose biosynthesis. 2. Deletion of the treYZ operon abolishes detectable trehalose. 3. Loss of the operon causes major reduction in P. aeruginosa growth in Arabidopsis, rescued by exogenous trehalose.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes maltooligosyltrehalose to release free trehalose from the terminal disaccharide
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; experimentally supported as essential component of treYZ branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: treZ disruption eliminates detectable trehalose and reduces virulence in Arabidopsis (~16-fold decrease in planta growth for transposon mutant), demonstrating positive control of trehalose-dependent pathogenicity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transposon insertion mutant; in planta bacterial growth assay; trehalose quantification
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports growth/virulence of P. aeruginosa in Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arabidopsis leaf infection; exogenous trehalose rescue at operon level
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for blocking pathogen trehalose output during plant infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First two genes in treYZ operon include glgA and treZ; transposon phenotype supports operon importance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. treZ encodes the trehalohydrolase step of the treYZ pathway. 2. A treZ insertion mutant produced no detectable trehalose. 3. treZ loss significantly impaired Arabidopsis infection.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of a 42.23 kb region containing trehalose biosynthetic operons
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/positive regulation inferred from reduced expression in mvfR mutant
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PA14_36375-36830 region including PA14_36500; treYZ and treS operon-containing cluster (co-regulated region)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of the 42.23 kb cluster; cellulase/peptidase PA14_36500 expression is reduced in mvfR mutant in planta
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream positive regulator linked to expression of the trehalose-associated cluster, but not directly validated as the focal determinant of trehalose output in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for maximum PA14 virulence in Arabidopsis and associated with reduced expression of the trehalose-linked genomic region when mutated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior mutant virulence evidence cited; in vitro transcriptional profiling reference; semiquantitative RT-PCR of PA14_36500 in planta
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: In vitro transcriptional profiling of mvfR-regulated genes; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Broad anti-virulence target, though not specifically a nutrient-metabolism core gene in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as contextual regulator of the gene cluster, but trehalose biosynthetic genes are the primary experimental focus
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mvfR positively regulates expression of the 42.23 kb region containing trehalose biosynthetic operons. 2. An mvfR mutant shows reduced PA14_36500 expression in planta. 3. mvfR is required for maximal Arabidopsis virulence, but is not the central metabolism gene experimentally dissected here.
